Title: A 22-nt artificial microRNA mediates widespread RNA silencing in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and seed coats retained anthocyanin when RDR6 was defective
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RDR6 is required for 22-nt amiRNA-mediated widespread CHS silencing because loss of RDR6 abolished phasiRNA accumulation and restricted silencing to local tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic crossing with rdr6 mutant; F2 genotyping/selection; northern blot showing absence of phasiRNAs; phenotype analysis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SSU promoter used to drive amiR22-CHS transgene upstream of RDR6-dependent effect
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights requirement for intact secondary siRNA/phasiRNA machinery when deploying 22-nt amiRNA strategies for broad metabolic silencing
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without RDR6, systemic/widespread silencing is lost
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 22-nt amiRNA-mediated widespread CHS silencing depended on RDR6 activity. 2. rdr6 mutants retained local silencing but lost root and seed-coat silencing. 3. phasiRNAs were undetectable in rdr6 plants, demonstrating that RDR6-catalyzed dsRNA production underlies the widespread effect.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is experimentally essential for secondary silencing amplification, but it is not a metabolic enzyme or transcription factor.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Col-0; pap1-D background used for overexpression context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hypocotyl; young leaves; petioles; roots; seed coat; rosette leaves depending on genotype/background
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies essential precursor for anthocyanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing of CHS reduces anthocyanin accumulation; 22-nt amiRNA-triggered CHS silencing caused widespread loss of anthocyanin including in roots and seed coats
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in pap1-D background; endogenous transcript abundance higher in pap1-D than Col-0 as inferred from stronger phasiRNA production
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: None beyond transgene-mediated silencing and genetic background
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry-step enzyme for anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthesis; treated as decisive visual marker but not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of anthocyanin pigmentation in multiple tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hpRNA silencing; 21-nt amiRNA silencing; 22-nt amiRNA silencing; northern blot of siRNAs/phasiRNAs; small RNA sequencing; phenotype observation in Col-0 and pap1-D; genetic test with rdr6 background
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SSU promoter (Arabidopsis RuBisCo small subunit 1A promoter, tissue-restricted); 35S promoter (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RDR6 (required indirectly for phasiRNA amplification from CHS transcript)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful proof-of-concept endogenous target for testing efficient silencing technologies to modulate flavonoid pigmentation traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS was the endogenous metabolic target used to compare hpRNA, 21-nt amiRNA, and 22-nt amiRNA silencing. 2. hpRNA and 21-nt amiRNA mainly caused local/aerial CHS silencing, whereas 22-nt amiRNA caused broader silencing including roots and seed coats in many lines. 3. Widespread CHS silencing correlated with RDR6-dependent phasiRNA accumulation from the endogenous CHS transcript.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CHS is the main nutrient-metabolism-relevant core gene assayed in Results; the study is a technical advance focused on silencing strategy rather than native CHS regulation.
